McCullough (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1347, aptly states, "a malicious prosecution plaintiff should be required to simply wait until it obtains a favorable termination in the entire underlying action" to avoid the "virtual certain[ty]" of creating apparent conflicts of attorney interest. Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James B. Kraus, Esq. of Law Offices of Andrew Zacks, (415-956-8100), for The Karsant Family Limited Partnership, Defendant and moving party.
Nick Reckas, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415-464-0913), for Nicholas Panoutsopoulos, Plaintiff and opposing party.

SUBMITTED. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE KARSANT FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint, read in the light most favorable to Plaintiff, alleges causation and existence of a qualified buyer/tenant. Plaintiff is not estopped from asserting damages caused by events occurring after April 2013, and demurrer may not be sustained to only a part of a cause of action re: pre April 2013 events. Defendants do not cite any authority to support their position that under the circumstances of this settlement agreement defendant's obligations to negotiate were limited to one time with one prospective buyer. Settlement Agreement was entered into on September 23, 2009 and contemplated submission of "any prospective purchasers" through September 14, 2013. (Settlement Agreement paragraph 6(b)). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge A. James Robertson, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
